    "noSurprisesFlags": [
      "Patient is self-pay with no insurance documentation; No Surprises Act protections may not apply but emergency care balance-billing restrictions should be verified",
      "Insurance allowed amount ($1,420.00) is significantly less than total billed ($6,915.00), indicating substantial write-off; verify this is a negotiated rate or standard contractual adjustment"
    ],
    "redFlags": [
      "DUPLICATE CHARGE: CPT 99284 (ED visit, level 4) billed twice on same date (2026-04-15) for $2,850.00 each; this appears to be a billing error unless two separate ED visits occurred",
      "DUPLICATE CHARGE: CPT J3010 (Fentanyl 0.1mg) billed twice for $80.00 each; verify if patient received two doses or if this is a duplicate entry",
      "HIGH FACILITY FEE: $500.00 facility fee represents 7.2% of total billed charges; combined with the emergency room setting, this is typical but warrants verification that it was not double-billed",
      "MISSING INSURANCE INFORMATION: Bill shows $1,420.00 insurance allowed but lists patient as 'self-pay' and shows no insurance name; unclear who determined allowed amount or whether insurance actually denied coverage",
      "UNALLOCATED PATIENT RESPONSIBILITY: Only $1,420.00 of the $6,915.00 total is listed as patient responsibility; the remaining $5,495.00 in adjustments is not explained—verify whether this is a contractual write-off, denial, or other adjustment",
      "CPT 99284 is emergency visit level 4; verify clinical documentation supports this level of complexity; overuse of level 4/5 codes in emergency departments is common upcoding pattern"
    ],
    "disputeActions": [
      "Contact Mercy Hospital billing department immediately and request: (1) itemized explanation of the $5,495.00 adjustment amount, (2) clarification on insurance allowed amount given patient is listed as self-pay, (3) detailed justification for duplicate CPT 99284 and CPT J3010 charges",
      "Request full itemized bill with medical record documentation for the ED visit to verify CPT 99284 level 4 complexity is clinically appropriate",
      "If only one ED visit occurred on 2026-04-15, demand removal of the duplicate $2,850.00 charge and recalculation of patient responsibility",
      "If only one dose of Fentanyl was administered, demand removal of duplicate $80.00 charge",
      "Request copy of the insurance EOB or explanation showing the $1,420.00 allowed amount; if no insurance is involved, ask Mercy Hospital to provide their self-pay discount rate and apply it",
      "For the chest X-ray (CPT 71046, $325.00): obtain quotes from freestanding imaging centers in Charlotte area; hospital ED radiology typically charges 40-60% more than independent imaging centers; request transfer of imaging to lower-cost facility and demand credit",
      "If you have insurance: file appeal with insurance company claiming duplicate billing and request re-review of ED visit level coding; attach clinical notes demonstrating whether level 4 was appropriate",
      "Document all phone calls with Mercy Hospital including date, time, and representative name; follow up any verbal agreements in writing via certified mail"
    ],
    "summary": "John Doe received emergency department care at Mercy Hospital on 2026-04-15 totaling $6,915.00 in charges. The bill contains two critical errors: CPT 99284 (ED visit level 4) is billed twice for $2,850.00 each, and CPT J3010 (Fentanyl) is billed twice for $80.00 each—these appear to be duplicate line items unless two separate ED visits or doses occurred, which is not indicated on the bill. The insurance allowed amount of $1,420.00 is 79.5% below total charges, but the bill confusingly lists the patient as self-pay with no insurance name provided, making it unclear whether this represents a contractual discount, insurance denial, or other adjustment. The $500.00 facility fee is appropriate for an emergency room setting. The primary concerns are the duplicate charges (potentially adding $2,930.00 in unnecessary billing), the unexplained discrepancy between 'self-pay' status and an 'insurance allowed' amount, and the absence of clear documentation showing how the patient's $1,420.00 responsibility was calculated. The high emergency room facility markup (typical for hospitals, roughly 2-3x what an urgent care would charge) is standard but the errors must be corrected before the patient pays any amount. The patient should contact billing immediately to resolve duplicates and obtain clarity on insurance status and responsibility allocation.",

  "summary_for_llm": "Total billed: $6,915.\nPatient owes: $1,420.\nInsurance paid: $0.\nFacility type: emergency room.\n8 line item(s) extracted.\n6 red flag(s) identified:\n  - DUPLICATE CHARGE: CPT 99284 (ED visit, level 4) billed twice on same date (2026-04-15) for $2,850.00 each; this appears to be a billing error unless two separate ED visits occurred\n  - DUPLICATE CHARGE: CPT J3010 (Fentanyl 0.1mg) billed twice for $80.00 each; verify if patient received two doses or if this is a duplicate entry\n  - HIGH FACILITY FEE: $500.00 facility fee represents 7.2% of total billed charges; combined with the emergency room setting, this is typical but warrants verification that it was not double-billed\n  - MISSING INSURANCE INFORMATION: Bill shows $1,420.00 insurance allowed but lists patient as 'self-pay' and shows no insurance name; unclear who determined allowed amount or whether insurance actually denied coverage\n  - UNALLOCATED PATIENT RESPONSIBILITY: Only $1,420.00 of the $6,915.00 total is listed as patient responsibility; the remaining $5,495.00 in adjustments is not explained—verify whether this is a contractual write-off, denial, or other adjustment\nDispute actions:\n  - Contact Mercy Hospital billing department immediately and request: (1) itemized explanation of the $5,495.00 adjustment amount, (2) clarification on insurance allowed amount given patient is listed as self-pay, (3) detailed justification for duplicate CPT 99284 and CPT J3010 charges\n  - Request full itemized bill with medical record documentation for the ED visit to verify CPT 99284 level 4 complexity is clinically appropriate\n  - If only one ED visit occurred on 2026-04-15, demand removal of the duplicate $2,850.00 charge and recalculation of patient responsibility\n  - If only one dose of Fentanyl was administered, demand removal of duplicate $80.00 charge\n  - Request copy of the insurance EOB or explanation showing the $1,420.00 allowed amount; if no insurance is involved, ask Mercy Hospital to provide their self-pay discount rate and apply it\n\nSummary: John Doe received emergency department care at Mercy Hospital on 2026-04-15 totaling $6,915.00 in charges. The bill contains two critical errors: CPT 99284 (ED visit level 4) is billed twice for $2,850.00 each, and CPT J3010 (Fentanyl) is billed twice for $80.00 each—these appear to be duplicate line items unless two separate ED visits or doses occurred, which is not indicated on the bill. The insurance allowed amount of $1,420.00 is 79.5% below total charges, but the bill confusingly lists the patient as self-pay with no insurance name provided, making it unclear whether this represents a contractual discount, insurance denial, or other adjustment. The $500.00 facility fee is appropriate for an emergency room setting. The primary concerns are the duplicate charges (potentially adding $2,930.00 in unnecessary billing), the unexplained discrepancy between 'self-pay' status and an 'insurance allowed' amount, and the absence of clear documentation showing how the patient's $1,420.00 responsibility was calculated. The high emergency room facility markup (typical for hospitals, roughly 2-3x what an urgent care would charge) is standard but the errors must be corrected before the patient pays any amount. The patient should contact billing immediately to resolve duplicates and obtain clarity on insurance status and responsibility allocation."
